diagnostics
zentoring/lib/app_web/components/core_components.ex
```elixir
defmodule AppWeb.CoreComponents do
// error: ** (CompileError) lib/app_web/components/core_components.ex: cannot compile module AppWeb.CoreComponents (errors have been logged)
  @moduledoc """
  Provides core UI components.
```
```elixir
    <div class="flex flex-col gap-3 mt-6">
      <.link
        navigate={~p"/oauth/google"}
// error: undefined function sigil_p/2 (expected AppWeb.CoreComponents to define such a function or for it to be imported, but none are available)
        class="flex items-center justify-center gap-2 rounded-lg bg-white px-4 py-2 text-sm font-semibold text-gray-700 shadow-sm ring-1 ring-gray-300 hover:bg-gray-50 w-full"
      >
```
```elixir

      <.link
        navigate={~p"/oauth/apple"}
// error: undefined function sigil_p/2 (expected AppWeb.CoreComponents to define such a function or for it to be imported, but none are available)
        class="flex items-center justify-center gap-2 rounded-lg bg-black px-4 py-2 text-sm font-semibold text-white shadow-sm hover:bg-gray-900 w-full"
      >
```

 correct problem with `~p` sigil
To fix the undefined `~p` sigil error, you need to import it from the `Phoenix.VerifiedRoutes` module. Add this import statement at the beginning of your `AppWeb.CoreComponents` module:

```elixir
defmodule AppWeb.CoreComponents do
  @moduledoc """
  Provides core UI components.
  """
  
  import Phoenix.VerifiedRoutes
  # ... rest of your module code
end
```

The `~p` sigil is used in Phoenix for verified routes, ensuring that the routes exist at compile time. This sigil is defined in `Phoenix.VerifiedRoutes`, so importing it will resolve the undefined function error.

Make sure your router module also has the following line near the top:

```elixir
use Phoenix.VerifiedRoutes
```

This will allow you to use the `~p` sigil for route generation throughout your application.
